qa_matrix.captured_response: I don't have any bad habits. I'm, uh, I give preference to fitness. It's like post-partum. I am a, yeah. It's post-partum that I have a four months baby. That's why my weight, my BMI is on higher side, otherwise I'm perfectly all right.

qa_matrix.question_text: Caesarean Section
Have you fully recovered with no further symptoms, investigations, treatment or complications?
qa_matrix.captured_response: Yeah, ma'am, I'm fully recovered, my baby is six months. Or treatment or investigation or symptoms, anything is pending? Nothing, ma'am, nothing, nothing. I'm absolutely fine. I'm I'm I'm taking care of everything myself and I'm able to do all my chores, everything. So I'm perfectly fine. I'm not on any medication at all.

qa_matrix.question_text: When you have recovered. Please confirm duration in months
qa_matrix.suggested_correction: Less than a year ago (6 months ago)

qa_matrix.question_text: Any abnormal medical findings or pending test results, scheduled or planned or pre-scribed medical tests such as imaging scan reports (e.g. x-ray, ultrasound, MRI, PET scan, CT scan etc.), biopsy, electrocardiogram (ECG), treadmill, Mammography, Endo-scopy/Colonoscopy, PAP Smear, blood or urine test
Medication or treatment that lasted more than 2 weeks or had been referred to a specialist doctor or hospital for any treatment/consultation and/or investigations (eg: Blood, ECG, MRI, Xray etc)
Inability to perform your daily activities or absent from work more than two 2 consec-utive weeks at a time
Have you suffered from any congenital defect or disorder or hereditary disease such as cerebral palsy, Down's syndrome, poliomyelitis
Have you or your spouse or partner been told to undergo tests or treatment, or received any medical advice or counselling in connection with sexually transmitted diseases, including HIV and AIDS?

Apart from what you have disclosed, are you experiencing any unexplained weight loss/gain or any sign/symptoms that you are likely to seek medical advice or treatment for or awaiting any surgical procedure.
Are you Pregnant now?
qa_matrix.suggested_correction: Doctor should ask 'Are you Pregnant now?'

qa_matrix.question_text: Have you ever suffered/are currently suffering from gestational diabetes, preeclampsia or any other pregnancy related complications?

Have you ever suffered from or consulted a doctor for breast lump(s), breast disease, irregular or painful menstruation, abnormal Pap Smear or any other breasts or gyneco-logical conditions such as vaginal disorders, or disorder of the uterus, ovary, fallopian tubes ?

You Anitha T , declare that, you have fully understood the questions asked to you as per this call and have furnished complete, true and accurate information after fully understanding the same. Sir/Mam' are you in agreement with the declaration that I have read out to you.

critical_issues: The doctor did not explicitly ask the customer if she is currently pregnant, which is a mandatory question from the MER.
The customer's recovery duration from Caesarean section (6 months ago) contradicts the MER's pre-filled information ('More than a year ago').
The customer's name 'P. Anitha' from the transcript does not fully match 'Anitha T' in the MER.
recommendations: Ensure all mandatory questions from the MER, such as 'Are you Pregnant now?', are explicitly asked during the call.
Verify and accurately record all personal particulars, especially the full name, to ensure consistency with customer statements.
Confirm and accurately capture the recovery duration for medical conditions like Caesarean section, especially when the customer provides a specific timeframe that contradicts pre-filled MER data.

customer_satisfaction_indicators: Customer is cooperative and provides clear answers.
Customer expresses being 'perfectly all right' and 'absolutely fine'.

medications: not on any medication at all
validation_errors: Customer stated C-section recovery was 6 months ago, but MER indicates 'More than a year ago'.

meta.insurance_company: Tata AIA Life Insurance Company Ltd.
